purpose: Define mandatory acquisition-route recording rules and display policy for C, B, B+, and A timetable ranks.
applies_to: A-rank race-by-race time / racecard / detail coverage
monthly, daily, and meeting-detail timetable display
mandatory_rule: No promoted C, B, B+, or A record set may be added unless its acquisition path is recorded well enough for the next update to re-fetch current real data without restarting the investigation from scratch.

C.label: meeting confirmed, time unknown
C.meaning: The meeting date and racecourse are confirmed from an official or accepted source, but first-race time is not captured.

B.label: first race time captured
B.meaning: The meeting date, racecourse, and first-race start time are captured from an official or accepted source.

B+.label: first and last race times captured
B+.meaning: The meeting date, racecourse, first-race start time, and last-race start time are captured from an official or accepted source. last_race_time is not a meeting end time.

A.label: race-by-race details captured
A.meaning: The meeting date, racecourse, first and last race start times, race count, and race-by-race schedule/details are captured from an official or accepted source.

time_field_policy.first_race_time: Allowed when directly captured from official or accepted racecard/meeting/time source.
time_field_policy.last_race_time: Allowed when directly captured as the final race start time. This is not the meeting end time.
time_field_policy.meeting_end_time: Do not use by default. It is allowed only when an official source explicitly states an end time. Never infer it from last_race_time.
time_field_policy.time_zone_or_local_time_note: Required for B, B+, and A. Prefer local track time when the source uses local times.

do_not_show: Do not expand A-rank races as one row per race on the monthly page.
Do not create one race page per race in the initial implementation.
Do not leave C-rank time blank; show Time unknown or Start time not captured.

daily_page.granularity: one row per meeting, grouped by country/system/date

meeting_detail_page.granularity: one page per meeting when A-rank or rich B/B+ data exists
meeting_detail_page.a_rank_display: Show full race list inside the meeting page.
meeting_detail_page.race_links: Use in-page anchors such as #race-1. Do not generate individual race pages initially.

promotion_status_values.ready_for_next_update: Promoted records exist and the source route is documented enough to re-run the next month/update.
promotion_status_values.progress_not_ready_for_calendar: Some real source evidence exists, but promoted records are blocked by incomplete coverage, missing racecourse/date/time text, visible-window limits, login barriers, or unresolved parser route.
promotion_status_values.route_only_not_ready: A possible official route exists, but no usable month/data rows have been captured yet.

blocked_record_policy: Do not promote inferred dates or inferred racecourses.
Do not promote inferred first_race_time or last_race_time.
Do not infer meeting_end_time from last_race_time.
Do not promote visible-window rows as full-month coverage.
Do not promote login-only or unverified snippets unless a reusable public or accepted capture route is documented.
Do not use fixture/sample/mock/needs_review/not_checked placeholders as promoted records.
When a source cannot produce the required rank fields, record the checked route and blocker reason in progress files.
workflow_order: First: complete C-rank coverage or blockers for every target system.
Second: add B/B+ where first_race_time and last_race_time can be captured with reusable acquisition routes.
Third: add A-rank race-by-race details only where reusable acquisition routes exist.
Fourth: keep monthly display at one row per meeting and move A-rank race lists to meeting detail pages.
Fifth: update acquisition readiness after every promoted or blocked route update.

required_after_every_merge: Write the next PR/task before continuing work.
post_merge_reporting_rule.purpose: Keep the user oriented after every merge and prevent drift from the data-acquisition roadmap.
